questions.specification: A watch manufacturer selects a specific typeface, a signature color palette, and a unique logo to be used consistently across its products and advertising to establish a recognizable personality. What is the collective term for these elements designed by the company?

The following table compares the specifications and market pricing of two competing high-end smartphones:

| Specification | Brand Alpha | Brand Beta |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | 
| Display | 6.7" Super AMOLED | 6.7" Super AMOLED |
| Storage Capacity | 256 GB | 256 GB |
| Camera Resolution | 108 MP | 108 MP |
| **Retail Market Price** | **$1,199** | **$799** |

The significant price premium that Brand Alpha is able to charge for a product with identical technical specifications to Brand Beta is a primary indicator of high:

Saffron Spring is a new start-up that sells canned sparkling tea in three flavours. The founders want to build a recognisable brand that can expand from local cafés to national supermarkets. They are redesigning their visual identity and packaging so customers can instantly identify the product on a crowded shelf.

![Figure 1: Three slim beverage cans on a supermarket shelf with bold colour blocks and a leaf-shaped icon, photographed front-on under bright retail lighting](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/d581650f-ed94-4c9e-8c07-26975adf54cb.png)

parts.content: Outline one element of Saffron Spring’s brand identity that would help customers recognise the brand quickly.
parts.markscheme:
- Identifies a valid brand-identity element (eg name, logo/mark, colour palette, typography, tagline, tone of voice, distinctive packaging shape) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Links the element to faster recognition/recall at point of sale or across channels (eg consistent use makes it easier to spot/remember) <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Outline how a brand positioning statement would help Saffron Spring’s design team make consistent packaging decisions.
parts.markscheme:
- States that positioning clarifies the target customer and the key point of difference/value proposition <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Explains that this guides consistent design choices (eg hierarchy of information, tone, colours, imagery) so all flavours look like one brand while still allowing variation <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain one positive impact and one negative impact of Saffron Spring using bold, high-contrast packaging.
parts.markscheme:
- Explains one positive impact on branding (eg increased visibility and brand awareness, improved recall, stronger perceived distinctiveness/premium cues, supports brand equity and preference) <marks>3 marks</marks>
- Explains one negative impact on branding (eg may conflict with desired brand personality like “calm/natural”, risks alienating target segment, could appear cheap/overly aggressive, may reduce trust if it looks inconsistent with product promise, could be confusing if too similar to competitors) <marks>3 marks</marks>

<note>Max 6 marks</note>
